subject: Create Production Dockerfile
description: Create Dockerfile with multi-stage build: Stage 1 (builder): FROM node:20-alpine, WORKDIR /app, COPY package*.json, RUN npm ci, COPY ., RUN npm run build:web. Stage 2 (production): FROM nginx:alpine, COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html, COPY nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf, EXPOSE 80, CMD [nginx, -g, daemon off;]. Create .dockerignore excluding node_modules, .git, .env, etc. Configure for arm64 build: use --platform linux/arm64 or multi-arch buildx. Test build locally with docker build -t worktracker . and docker run -p 3000:80 worktracker.
result_summary:
Created production containerization for the Expo web app. Added a multi-stage `Dockerfile` that builds the web export with `node:20-alpine` and serves `/app/dist` from `nginx:alpine`, with BuildKit `BUILDPLATFORM`/`TARGETPLATFORM` support so downstream deployment can build `linux/arm64` images via buildx without changing the file. Added `.dockerignore` to exclude local artifacts and secrets from the build context, and added a baseline `nginx/nginx.conf` with SPA fallback and a `/health` endpoint that downstream compose/proxy tasks can reuse.

Verification: `npm run typecheck` passed. `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed and exported `dist`. Attempted `docker build -t worktracker-task-039 .`, but local Docker validation was blocked by the environment (`cannot pull with rebase` was resolved separately; Docker failed with daemon connection error `broken pipe` to `http://docker.example.com/...`). `git pull --rebase` was attempted before commit and failed because the shared worktree had unrelated unstaged changes. Git commit created: `[task-039] Create production Dockerfile` (`3e2fe41`).

Definition of Done notes: input validation/auth/authz/error-path items were not applicable because this task only adds deployment artifacts, not runtime request handling. No automated happy/error-path tests exist for Docker artifacts in the repo yet; validation was limited to typecheck, web export, and the attempted Docker build.
